~ !  ~

Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
~ !  ~
65
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
1.
Three principles of cosmetic acupuncture
Even at the same acupoint, the practitioner’s choice and insertion of needle should differ
based on the purpose of needling.
If the purpose is regulating the channel based on TCM theory, it should be mostly
perpendicular and oblique insertion on the exact acupoint.
If  the  practitioner  wants  to  increase  the  circulation  or  tissue  formation  of  collagen
ﬁbroblast  for  example,  transverse  insertion  targeting  the  dermis  is  ideal  because  the
practitioner can stimulate a larger area with the transverse angle of insertion and don’t need
to insert deeply. Usually, we use the ﬁnest needles and many needles to cover more dermis
area. 5-6 needles insertion on nasolabial fold is a good example. The roller needle increases
the stimulation area on depth of the dermis.
If the practitioner’s purpose is releasing muscles and myofascia, the needle should be
inserted deep enough to reach the tightened point of the muscle, not the exact acupoint. In
this case, the needle diameter is relatively thicker compared to regular channel needling.
Depending on muscle size and location, perpendicular or transverse/oblique insertion can
be applied.
2.
Practitioner's skill level
More experienced practitioners can use thicker needles with less pain and the ﬁnest
needle with ﬁrmness. On the face, needling with needles ﬁner in diameter requires a lot of
practice because we have to control the very thin needle to keep it from getting bent.
Reaching the small, exact target muscle on the face is not easy work. At ﬁrst, practitioners
should use moderate size needles.
